name: Tax Surplus / ACB
tagline: Foreign-affiliate surplus pools and ACB, re-derived to a human gate
plain_summary: Tax Surplus / ACB rolls seeded fictional foreign-affiliate facts through per-entity, per-year surplus pools, a distribution waterfall, tier elevation, capital-event-only ACB, and per-layer FX. It emits traceable workpapers, independently rechecks structural identities, and can drive a drifted workpaper set back to a clean tie-out while reserving material changes for human review.
marketing_thesis: The public package exposes its intermediates, its reconciliation identities, and the exact commands behind the page. Its control claims can be re-run from the repository instead of accepted on presentation alone.
layers_intro: Each layer has one duty: source facts feed the calculation, the harness challenges its exposed intermediates, and the bounded loop stops at a human-review boundary.

layers.label: Human Gate
layers.plain: A material correction is shown to a person before sign-off; an unresolved run is escalated.
layers.engineering: The assurance loop maps convergence and total CAD adjustment magnitude to PASS, FLAG, or FAIL. FLAG and PASS exit 0; FAIL exits 1. FLAG explicitly requires human review of what changed.

layers.label: Assurance Loop
layers.plain: Finds the earliest broken fiscal period, restores it from source-derived results, and checks the whole structure again.
layers.engineering: observe -> detect -> remediate -> re-verify -> gate: each bounded turn settles the earliest failing year, books field-level adjustments, locks that period, and repeats until reconciliation passes or the budget is exhausted.

layers.label: Reconciliation Harness
layers.plain: Recomputes the structural rules from published intermediates and identifies the entity, year, and amount of any break.
layers.engineering: reconcile.py independently checks pool and ACB conservation, waterfall limits, non-negative balances, roll-forward continuity, elevation, and the functional-currency ACB-layer tie-out. The --check CLI flag returns non-zero on a break.

layers.label: Surplus / ACB Engine
layers.plain: Calculates each entity and year in order, carrying tax pools forward and moving ACB only for capital events.
layers.engineering: SurplusEngine processes entities bottom-up within each year: income plus the Reg. 5907(2) adjustment, exempt/taxable split, distribution-triggered elevation at ownership percentage, exempt-taxable-pre-acquisition waterfall, capital-event ACB, and closing-to-opening roll-forward.

layers.label: Seeded Fictional Facts
layers.plain: Creates a reproducible multi-tier example without client or employer data.
layers.engineering: generate_structure builds the fictional entities, fiscal-year facts, ownership chain, and FX table from DEFAULT_SEED = 20240607 unless --seed selects another reproducible run.

blocks.label: Fictional Source Facts
blocks.plain: seeded entities, years, ownership, capital events, distributions, and FX
blocks.engineering: generate_structure returns Structure plus YearFacts from a reproducible seed

blocks.label: SurplusEngine
blocks.plain: rolls surplus pools and ACB forward
blocks.engineering: bottom-up yearly computation; waterfall, elevation, ACB, and closing balances

blocks.label: Workpapers + Workbook
blocks.plain: writes per-entity workpapers, summaries, FX analysis, reconciliation, and optional Excel
blocks.engineering: report.py and workbook.py render the engine's exposed inputs, intermediates, and balances

blocks.label: Independent Reconciliation
blocks.plain: re-derives the identities and reports any break
blocks.engineering: named checks compare expected and actual values within the implemented tolerance

blocks.label: Bounded Remediation Loop
blocks.plain: settles the earliest failing year and rechecks
blocks.engineering: replaces drifted rows with authoritative engine results, journals field changes, locks the year, and repeats within a turn budget

blocks.label: Human Review Boundary
blocks.plain: reviews material changes or an unresolved failure
blocks.engineering: FLAG is converged but material and requires review before sign-off; FAIL is a non-converged escalation

spec_strip.label: Tests
spec_strip.source: py -3 -m pytest --collect-only -q -o addopts= (tax-surplus-engine/); verified 2026-07-20 and pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json
spec_strip.plain: the collected checks that pin this engine's behavior

spec_strip.label: Default run
spec_strip.source: tax-surplus-engine/surplus_engine/cli.py -- --start defaults to 2021 and --end defaults to 2024

spec_strip.label: Default reconciliation
spec_strip.plain: identity-check instances across the default structure

spec_strip.label: Loop verdicts
spec_strip.plain: PASS and FLAG exit 0; FAIL exits 1

problem_statement: Foreign-affiliate surplus and adjusted cost base are not one ending balance. They are connected roll-forwards across entities, years, ownership percentages, distributions, capital events, and exchange-rate layers. A spreadsheet can still look orderly while a stale opening, unsupported elevation, overdrawn pool, or mis-keyed ACB breaks the structure. This engine makes those mechanics explicit and gives a separate harness enough exposed detail to re-derive them.

scenarios.title: A clean default run proves every implemented identity
scenarios.narrative: The seeded FY2021-2024 structure runs through the engine and the --check gate. The current repository reports Reconciliation: OK with 212 of 212 identity-check instances passing and exits 0.

scenarios.title: Per-layer FX exposes a basis result hidden by one closing rate
scenarios.narrative: In the seeded Cedar Mezz example, a 2023 contribution of 35,502.71 is returned in 2024 at a different rate. Closing USD ACB and the single-rate CAD amount are both zero, while the event-by-event CAD layers net to (660.35). The functional-currency layers still tie to the engine.

scenarios.title: The demo clears seeded drift but does not conceal material corrections
scenarios.narrative: The built-in loop profile starts with six reconciliation breaks. It locks FY2021, FY2022, and FY2023 in three turns, reducing breaks 6 -> 4 -> 2 -> 0. Four booked adjustments total CAD 107,088.50, above the CAD 1,000.00 default materiality, so the converged run returns FLAG for human review and exits 0.
scenarios.source: py -3 -m surplus_engine.loop --demo (tax-surplus-engine/); reproduced 2026-07-20; surplus_engine/loop.py -- DEMO_FAULTS and DEFAULT_MATERIALITY_CAD

benchmarks.source: py -3 -m pytest --collect-only -q -o addopts= (tax-surplus-engine/); verified 2026-07-20 and pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json

benchmarks.label: Per-layer FX sign difference
benchmarks.unit: per-layer ACB versus CAD 0.00 at one closing rate
benchmarks.source: tax-surplus-engine/README.md -- seeded Cedar Mezz per-layer FX example; surplus_engine/fx.py

verdict_map.severity: Converged; booked adjustments at or below materiality
verdict_map.action: clean to present for sign-off; exits 0
human reviews what changed before sign-off; exits 0

verdict_map.severity: Not converged within the turn budget
verdict_map.action: escalate with structural breaks unresolved; exits 1

guarantees: Seeded fictional inputs make the public run reproducible without client or employer data.
Surplus elevation occurs only when a subsidiary distributes and only at its ownership percentage; operating income alone does not elevate.
ACB changes only for capital contributions and applied returns of capital; an excess return surfaces as a deemed gain and ACB is floored at zero.
The reconciliation harness independently recomputes conservation, waterfall, continuity, elevation, and ACB FX-layer identities from exposed intermediates.
A settled loop result is built from the same authoritative engine re-derivation as a clean run, with every changed tracked field recorded as an adjustment.

gate_policy.demo_gate: PASS and FLAG exit 0; FAIL exits 1
gate_policy.note: The loop can restore structural consistency, but a FLAG requires a person to review material booked adjustments before sign-off, and a FAIL escalates without claiming convergence.

limits.statement: The repository package is a fictional reference implementation over generated entities, facts, and FX rates; it does not reproduce a client or employer methodology or dataset.
The default 0.60 exempt-distribution cap is explicitly a fictional, configurable modeling constraint, not a claim that one ceiling applies to every real tax fact pattern.
The --check gate proves the structural identities implemented in reconcile.py within its 0.02 tolerance; it does not replace tax judgment or human approval.
limits.source: tax-surplus-engine/surplus_engine/reconcile.py -- DEFAULT_TOLERANCE and reconcile; README.md -- human gate

limits.statement: The loop remediates only by restoring source-derived engine values. It does not invent support for a value, and a non-converged run returns FAIL for escalation.
